articleBody: El petróleo Brent visita de nuevo la zona del 119,00, nivel que estuvo actuando como resistencia durante la fuerte subida que tuvo el commodity durante el 9 de marzo de este año. Aparentemente el 119,00 ha vuelto a actuar como resistencia para el Brent y desde ese nivel rebota de nuevo a la baja, hacia la zona del 106,20. Sin embargo, pese al retroceso bajista en el Brent, la tendencia alcista a corto y medio plazo todavía se mantiene y es posible que regrese al alza, especialmente cuando los mínimos de las velas diarias vienen siendo más altos que los anteriores. De retroceder un poco más a la baja, la zona de los 100 dólares por barril podría actuar como soporte para el Brent, pero con los mínimos de las velas diarias siendo más altos que los anteriores, la presión se está acumulando al alza, precisamente en la resistencia del 119,00. Lo que principalmente ha estado apoyando al Brent y al petróleo WTI últimamente ha sido el conflicto en Oriente Medio. De mantenerse las interrupciones de producción y transporte de crudo en Oriente Medio, es posible que el Brent llegue a romper por encima del 119,00. Por otro lado, si la guerra con Irán llega a calmarse y se logra un cese de las hostilidades en la región, el Brente podría regresar por debajo del 100,00 y por debajo de ese nivel, el próximo soporte podría estar en la zona del 80,00, por donde está la media móvil exponencial de 55 días. La EMA34 se mantiene por debajo de la EMA89 → la tendencia principal no se ha revertido, pero a corto plazo se espera un retroceso hacia la zona de oferta superior. Plan principal: Comprar a corto plazo en el rebote. Priorizar la compra en zonas de soporte/correcciones menores. Atualmente, o preço está a acumular-se lateralmente logo abaixo da área intermédia do canal, formando uma zona de compressão de liquidez. Muitas vezes, isto é um sinal de uma rutura contínua na tendência principal. Os recuos anteriores foram todos rejeitados na borda superior do canal e não houve sinais de quebra estrutural → mostrando que os vendedores ainda estão no controlo. Em termos macro, o contexto actual é ainda favorável ao dólar, uma vez que o mercado mantém a expectativa de que a Reserva Federal não terá pressa em abrandar a política. A manutenção de rendimentos elevados coloca o ouro sob pressão no curto prazo, limitando a possibilidade de uma forte recuperação. O preço-alvo visa o fundo do canal em torno de 4.840, coincidindo com a zona de liquidez inferior marcada.

articleBody: Bitcoin ha vuelto a reaccionar desde la zona de demanda de 71.000 a 71.300, validando la estructura anterior tras la caída impulsada por la presión macroeconómica de la Reserva Federal y el fortalecimiento del dólar estadounidense. Esta zona sigue actuando como una base de liquidez clave, donde los compradores están entrando tras el repunte impulsivo y la formación de CHOCH. Técnicamente, esto todavía parece un retroceso saludable dentro de una estructura alcista, no una reversión completa. El mercado arrastró la liquidez por debajo del soporte y aprovechó la demanda, lo que suele generar señales acumuladas antes de la continuación. Si esta zona se mantiene, BTC puede ganar impulso hacia 72.800 → 73.500 y, potencialmente, volver a probar la resistencia superior cerca de 74.000. Sin embargo, si el precio no logra mantenerse por encima de 71.000, indicaría debilidad y falta de fuerza compradora. En ese caso, el mercado podría buscar mayor liquidez en la siguiente zona de demanda importante (69.200 – 69.600), que coincide con la base de consolidación anterior y la zona de mayor interés institucional. Fundamentalmente, el BTC sigue siendo sensible al sentimiento de riesgo y a las condiciones de liquidez. Si bien las tensiones geopolíticas respaldan a las criptomonedas a largo plazo, los movimientos a corto plazo aún están fuertemente influenciados por las tasas de interés y la fortaleza del USD. Zonas clave: Soporte/Demanda: 71.000 – 71.300 Demanda principal: 69.200 – 69.600 Resistencia: 72.800 – 73.500 Escenarios: Mantenerse por encima de 71.000: Continuación alcista → 73.000+ Romper por debajo de 71.000: Caída hacia la zona de liquidez de 69.500

articleBody: Una vez tocado el target en el nivel 382, la onda 4 puede darse por terminada y las reglas liberan a los institucionales para dar inicio a la batalla entre osos y toros. Siendo Viernes, cabe esperar que los toros traten de ir al nivel.5 de fibo para tomar ganancias de fin de semana, pero los osos una vez cumplida la regla de Eliot que rige a TODOS los traders profesionales que mueven el precio, intentaran llevar el precio al target de onda 5 menor que en este caso queda en la ZONA de 67.2 En este punto, los exchanges son quienes actúan para barrer SL y Liquidar sobre apalancados. justo ahora el precio esta en el nivel 618 desde donde una vez liquidados los longs sobre apalancados, puede ir a buscar el siguiente target de onda 4 al.5 o seguir su curso al mínimo anterior y buscar la onda plana o seguir al target de onda 5 menor. Esas son la probabilidades, así son de amplias las reglas de Eliot, o no tendría ningún chiste ser trader y todos tendríamos la bola de cristal para saber que van a hacer los que lo mueven, no habría transferencia de capital entre perdedores y ganadores. Tu estas en tu derecho de creer que esto es un cuento para dormir, o también tienes la oportunidad de aprender al menos reglas básicas de Eliot y comprobar por ti mismo como funcionan las ZONAS y en que momentos se da la batalla y comienza la volatilidad y con ello oportunidades de ganar dinero... o perder SL y peor aun, ser liquidado todo tu capital. Es básico y muy importante que sepas usar correctamente las herramientas de fibonacci, o tus ZONAS van a estar fuera de rango. Nosotros No sabemos que piensan hacer quienes lo mueven, así que vamos a probabilidades en base a confirmaciones. Si pierde el 618, es mas probable que siga bajando, si recupera el 382, de los que están en blanco, puede ir al segundo target. Mi long se cerro por TP en el target uno el 382 azul, Así que por ahora espero confirmaciones para operar lo que la acción del precio muestre como mayor probabilidad. Dejo enlace a Grafico completo.

title: ¿Qué es un patrón de cuña? para WHITEBIT:BTCUSDT de WhiteBIT
description: ¡Hola, traders! 👋 Los patrones en cuña son una herramienta poderosa en el análisis técnico que puede darte una idea de los posibles movimientos de los precios. listing_text: ¡Hola, traders! 👋 Los patrones en cuña son una herramienta poderosa en el análisis técnico que puede darte una idea de los posibles movimientos de los precios. Tanto si detectas una cuña descendente como si detectas una ascendente, estas formaciones pueden revelar señales clave sobre la dirección del mercado. ¿Qué es un patrón de cuña? Un patrón de cuña se forma cuando el precio se mueve entre dos líneas de tendencia convergentes, creando una forma similar a un triángulo. Estos patrones suelen aparecer cuando el mercado se ralentiza o se consolida antes de un movimiento más importante. Las cuñas pueden inclinarse hacia arriba o hacia abajo, y la diferencia clave radica en si las líneas de tendencia convergen hacia una tendencia alcista (cuña ascendente) o hacia una tendencia bajista (cuña descendente). Patrón de cuña descendente: reversión alcista 📈 El patrón de cuña descendente es una señal de reversión alcista. Esta formación se produce cuando el precio se mueve entre dos líneas de tendencia descendentes, generando una serie de máximos y mínimos cada vez más bajos. El impulso bajista se debilita a medida que las líneas de tendencia convergen, lo que indica que los vendedores están perdiendo fuerza, lo que crea el potencial para una ruptura alcista. Cómo comerciar con la cuña descendente Entrada: Espera a que el precio rompa por encima de la línea de tendencia superior. Esta es tu señal para entrar en largo. Objetivo: Mide la altura de la cuña en su punto más ancho y proyecta esta altura hacia arriba desde el punto de ruptura. Stop Loss: Colócalo justo por debajo del mínimo más reciente para proteger tu posición si no se produce una ruptura. El gráfico ilustra un patrón de cuña descendente en el par Bitcoin/Tether US con un marco temporal de 1 hora. La acción del precio se encuentra contenida entre dos líneas de tendencia convergentes con pendiente descendente, lo que sugiere un debilitamiento del impulso bajista. La ruptura por encima de la línea de tendencia superior indica una reversión alcista y el posterior repunte del precio confirma el cambio de impulso. En casos excepcionales, una ruptura fallida puede dar lugar a un patrón de cuña descendente bajista, aunque este escenario es menos común. Mantén un ojo en la acción del precio para detectar señales de un impulso alcista continuo. Patrón de cuña ascendente: reversión bajista 📉 El patrón de cuña ascendente es una señal de reversión bajista. Esta formación se produce cuando el precio se mueve entre dos líneas de tendencia ascendentes, generando máximos y mínimos cada vez más altos. La cuña ascendente indica un debilitamiento de la presión compradora y una posible reversión a la baja. Cómo comerciar con la cuña ascendente Entrada: Entra en una posición corta una vez que el precio rompa por debajo de la línea de tendencia inferior. Objetivo: Mide la altura de la cuña y proyecta la altura desde el punto de ruptura hacia abajo. Stop Loss: Establece el stop loss justo por encima del máximo más reciente para proteger tu posición. Comerciar con el patrón gráfico de cuña: consejos clave ⚡ El contexto lo es todo cuando se comercia con patrones de cuña. Si aparece un patrón de cuña alcista en una tendencia alcista, es más probable que se rompa al alza. Si aparece una cuña bajista en una tendencia bajista, es de esperar una ruptura. Aquí tienes algunos consejos rápidos para mejorar tu estrategia de trading con patrones de cuña: Las líneas de tendencia son clave: asegúrate de trazarlas con precisión. Unas líneas de tendencia bien trazadas te permitirán realizar operaciones más eficaces. Confirmación de la ruptura: confirma las rupturas con un aumento del volumen y, de ser posible, comprobando la confluencia con otros indicadores, como el RSI o el MACD. Una ruptura sin volumen suele ser una señal falsa. Gestión del riesgo: utiliza siempre un stop loss para proteger tu capital. Utiliza otros indicadores: los patrones de cuña funcionan bien junto con herramientas adicionales como el RSI, las medias móviles o el MACD. Cuanta más confluencia, mejor. Reflexiones finales 🏁 Los patrones de cuña, ya sea el patrón de cuña descendente que indica una reversión alcista o el patrón de cuña ascendente que indica presión bajista, son algunas de las formaciones gráficas más fiables ​​que existen. Pero recuerda: ninguna configuración es perfecta, así que utiliza siempre un stop loss y nunca confíes en un solo indicador. Con la práctica, mejorarás en la detección de estas configuraciones y en la sincronización de tus entradas y salidas como un profesional. ¡Feliz trading y que los gráficos te sean favorables! 💰📊

Recent price action suggests a resistance retest, potentially setting up for another move lower if resistance holds. Key Level: 3000 This zone, previously a consolidation area, now acts as a significant resistance level. A failed test and rejection at 3000 would likely resume the bearish momentum. Downside targets include: 2590 – Initial support 2490 – Intermediate support 2300 – Longer-term support level Bullish Scenario (breakout above 3000): A confirmed breakout and daily close above 3000 would invalidate the bearish setup. articleBody: AUDUSD has reversed sharply to the downside into the end of the week, with price action increasingly driven by broad-based US dollar strength. The recent shift in momentum suggests that the prior upside move was temporary, with sellers now regaining control. From an Elliott Wave perspective, the internal structure supports this bearish turn. The recovery from the 0.6947 low unfolded in three waves, indicating that the move was corrective rather than impulsive. This strongly suggests it was a wave B, rather than the start of a sustained bullish trend. The brief push to new highs now appears to have been a false breakout, followed by a sharp reversal lower. This type of price action fits well with the idea of an irregular correction, where wave B exceeds the origin of wave A before the market resumes to the downside. As a result, the broader structure may still be developing as a wave four correction, but one that is more complex than initially expected. If this scenario plays out, the correction could take the form of a triangle or a flat pattern. In either case, attention should be placed on potential support zones that may come into play once the structure matures—particularly after a possible wave E in a triangle scenario. Key levels to watch include the previous wave A swing low around 0.6947, followed by the 0.6900 psychological level. This area is further supported by the presence of a prior wave four of a lesser degree, making it a technically significant support zone. Despite the current downside pressure, this zone could attract buyers and stabilize price action, potentially still within this month. From a fundamental perspective, a relatively hawkish stance from the Reserve Bank of Australia due to persistent inflation concerns could continue to support the Australian dollar and help maintain the broader uptrend once this corrective phase is complete.
listing_text: Amazon is moving lower as expected, continuing its bearish trajectory after a clear breakdown below the corrective channel. The decline has been impulsive, with price now trading well below the 227 level, reinforcing the presence of strong selling pressure. From an Elliott Wave perspective, the move can still be interpreted as an extended wave C. However, the structure appears incomplete, as five distinct subwaves have yet to fully develop. This suggests that the current decline may extend further before reaching a meaningful bottom. Looking ahead, key retracement levels come into focus. The 0.618 Fibonacci retracement near 193 and the 0.786 level around 178 represent potential downside targets. These zones could gradually form a more attractive support area where a rebound may develop. That said, bearish momentum remains firmly in control. In the short term, traders should be prepared for continued downside within subwave “v” of (C) before any significant recovery takes place. Highlights: • Wave C breakdown below corrective channel confirms weakness • 193–178 area can be attractive for a rebound

Instead of completely falling apart, it’s building a really clean consolidation base right around the $80–$100 level. Look at the volume profile at the bottom during this recent chop. This is exactly the kind of "coiling" action you want to see before a breakout. It’s sitting on an RS Rating of 98. You can see the "SL" (Stop Loss) marked right below the recent lows of this consolidation zone. Because of that 9.3% ADR, you can size this so a stop-out is just a paper cut, but a breakout is a home run.

articleBody: AUDUSD on the daily chart is consolidating after a strong impulsive move higher, with price now compressing into a symmetrical triangle near the 0.70 area. The structure shows a series of lower highs pressing against a rising support line, suggesting the market is in a pause phase rather than a clear reversal at this stage. The moving averages continue to support the broader constructive backdrop. Price remains above both the 50-day SMA and the 200-day SMA, while the 50-day is still trending above the 200-day, reinforcing that the larger trend structure remains positive. The pullback into consolidation appears controlled so far, with no decisive breakdown beneath the rising average support. Momentum has cooled, which fits the consolidation theme. MACD has flattened and drifted lower from its earlier peak, indicating the prior upside momentum has slowed. RSI is hovering around the midline near 50, showing a more neutral balance between buyers and sellers and confirming the lack of immediate directional expansion. As long as price holds above the triangle’s lower boundary and the 50-day SMA, the chart still leans constructive on a broader basis, though the short-term picture is clearly neutral while this range tightens. A decisive move outside the triangle would likely define the next directional phase, with traders watching whether the pair resumes the prior uptrend or slips into a deeper retracement. Overall, AUDUSD is showing consolidation within an intact medium-term uptrend, with triangle compression, moving average support, and neutralizing momentum all pointing to a market that is preparing for its next larger move. -MW

articleBody: 📊[b] Alibaba (BABA) – Technical Setup & Investment View[/b] Alibaba Group On a purely technical basis, we believe Alibaba presents a compelling buying opportunity at current levels. 🧠[b] Background[/b] The stock has declined significantly from its peak, primarily due to: Regulatory pressure in China (2021 crackdown) Slowing growth expectations Broader sentiment shift away from Chinese equities In 2021, Alibaba entered a major correction, breaking key support levels and falling back to price ranges last seen around 2015 (sub-$60 levels). 📉 [b]Structure Since Then[/b] Since bottoming, the stock: Entered a prolonged consolidation phase Built a strong base over multiple years Eventually broke out of this consolidation in 2025 Following the breakout, Alibaba traded within a clearly defined ascending channel (marked in purple), reflecting improving sentiment and trend strength. ⚠️ [b]Recent Price Action (March 2026)[/b] In March 2026, the stock broke below the lower boundary of the ascending channel. [image]https://www.tradingview.com/x/POt4o9bY/[/image] However, we view this not as structural weakness, but as: 👉 A natural retest of the prior consolidation resistance, which has now turned into a key support level. 📊 [b]Why the Stock Fell Recently[/b] The recent pullback appears to be driven by a combination of: Short-term profit-taking after the strong 2025 rally Broader volatility in Chinese equities Macro uncertainty (rates, global growth expectations) Profit Collapse: Adjusted net income fell to RMB 15.63–16.7 billion High Cost of Strategic Pivot 👉 Importantly, this move looks technical rather than fundamental deterioration 📈 [b]Current Setup[/b] Alibaba is now revisiting: 👉 A major support zone formed by prior consolidation resistance At the same time: Momentum remains constructive Sentiment is stabilizing Analysts continue to project upside 🎯 [b]Valuation Context[/b] Current analyst targets: High: ~$237 Low: ~$121–140 Median: ~$195 👉 This implies: Limited downside from current levels Attractive risk/reward profile 🔥 [b]Investment Thesis[/b] We believe: The current level represents a high-probability support zone The recent breakdown is likely a false move / retest The stock is positioned for a potential reversal ⚠️ [b]Strategy[/b] We are monitoring for: Stabilization at current levels Confirmation of support holding Early signs of trend reversal 👉 Upon confirmation, we view Alibaba as a strong buy candidate 🏁 [b]Final Take[/b] 👉 Alibaba is at a critical technical level where risk/reward is skewed to the upside We are not buying the breakout, we are buying the retest of strength. articleBody: WTI Crude Oil is back in the green for a third consecutive session, rebounding after an unprecedented 33% intraday crash from the $113.00 highs. The oil market is currently a battleground between massive geopolitical supply shocks and coordinated global efforts to flood the market with emergency reserves. With volatility remaining high, we break down the critical 50% Fibonacci level that will dictate whether oil returns to triple digits or continues its consolidation. Key topics covered - The supply shock: Escalating tensions, including the US targeting Iranian banks, attacks on Gulf tankers, and reports of Iran laying underwater mines in the Strait of Hormuz, are keeping a massive risk premium in the market. - SPR drawdown vs. global demand: The IEA has announced a record 400-million-barrel release, bolstered by President Trump authorising a 172-million-barrel drawdown from the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve over the next four months. However, with the Strait of Hormuz responsible for 20 million barrels of daily transit, this release only covers a fraction of the potential disruption. - The Macro breakout: We review the massive double (or triple) bottom breakout from the $55.00 level. WTI easily cleared the $77.00 neckline, achieving its 100% measured move target at $99.50 and spiking all the way to the 161.8% Fibonacci extension at $113.00 before dumping. - Momentum vs. structure: The daily RSI is hovering near 80. While heavily overbought, momentum can stay overbought in headline-driven markets. As long as price remains above the $77.00 neckline, the broader technical structure remains biased to the upside. WTI scenarios & trade plan: - Bearish (Short-term rejection): The pivot is the 50% Fibonacci retracement at $93.25. If WTI fails to break and hold it, the longer-term bias stays to the downside. Risk-takers fading the $93.25 resistance can expect a pullback to the 38.2% Fib at $84.20, and potentially a retest of the $76.50 - $77.00 neckline (likely driven by headlines of a truce or de-escalation). - Bullish (Return to triple digits): If buyers can break and hold above $93.25, it signals a sustained continuation. The immediate objective is filling Monday’s gap at the $99.50 open, with further upside potential toward the 61.8% Fib and a full duplication of the measured move pointing to $120.00. listing_text: CHoCH on alts: how I stop guessing “is this a reversal?” You know that moment on alts: price nukes for weeks, suddenly does a cute little bounce, Twitter screams “bottom in”, and you sit there thinking: “Is this THE reversal… or just another dead cat doing parkour?” Let’s talk about CHoCH – Change of Character – and why I love it as an early structure-shift signal so I don’t have to play reversal roulette. I’ll keep it in simple trader language, not textbook nonsense. What’s CHoCH in plain English? If trend is just “higher highs / higher lows” or “lower highs / lower lows” then CHoCH is the first clean moment when price says: “Yo, I’m not behaving like before.” Downtrend example on an alt: - Lower high - Lower low - Lower high - Lower low All good, bears are cruising. Then suddenly: - Price makes a low - Bounces - Comes back… but fails to make a new low - Instead, it breaks above the last lower high That break above the last lower high – that’s your CHoCH. Structure just “changed character” from clean bearish to “hmm, something’s off.” It’s not a full trend reversal confirmation, but it’s the first slap in the face: “Stop blindly shorting this, genius.” Why CHoCH is gold on alts Alts are drama queens: - Huge wicks - Fake breakouts - News spikes - Market makers farming stops like it’s harvest season If you just wait for a textbook uptrend (higher high, higher low, etc.), you’re usually late. CHoCH lets you: - See the first sign that sellers are losing control - Shift from full-on bearish bias to “ok, maybe rotation coming” - Start stalking long setups instead of shorting into the floor I’m not saying “buy every CHoCH”. I’m saying: “stop fading moves when structure tells you the party changed.” How I actually use CHoCH On alts, I mainly watch 1h and 4h. Lower than that, it’s mostly noise and pain. Basic flow for a bearish-to-bullish CHoCH: 1) Identify clear downtrend: obvious lower highs & lower lows. 2) Wait for price to form a low, then a bounce (a swing high). 3) Price revisits the lows but: - either makes a slightly higher low - or double-bottoms and launches 4) Key moment: price breaks above that “swing high” that formed on the bounce. That break = CHoCH. Downtrend behavior is broken. After CHoCH, I’m not instantly ape-long. I usually wait for: - A pullback into the zone that got broken (former lower high area) - Some reaction: wick rejections, smaller timeframe support holding - Then I plan entries with tight invalidation under the local low You’ll often see this on alts after brutal dumps: Smart money loads quietly while retail still screams “short every bounce”. CHoCH is the first visual clue they’re losing that fight. Bearish CHoCH works the same in reverse: - Uptrend with higher highs / higher lows - First time price fails to make a new high - Then breaks below the last higher low That’s the heads-up: “maybe stop buying every green candle, champ.” Where beginners mess up CHoCH 1) Calling every spike a CHoCH One random wick above a high is not structure shift. You need: - A clear prior trend - A proper swing high/swing low - Then a clean break of that swing 2) Forcing it on choppy ranges If price is just sideways, there’s no “character” to change. It’s just noise. 3) Treating CHoCH as 100% reversal confirmation It’s an early signal, not a holy grail. Maybe I’m wrong, but if you think one broken high = “new bull market”, the market will gladly educate you. How CHoCH helps your trading psychology The real win isn’t just “better entries”, it’s less emotional guessing. Instead of: - “Is this the bottom? Is it over? What if I miss it?” You switch to: - “Has structure changed? Yes/No.” It turns: - FOMO into a checklist - Panic into a plan - Gut feelings into visual, repeatable signals You stop buying every dip and shorting every rip, and start reacting to actual behavior shifts. Quick mental summary On alts, when you see: - Strong downtrend - First higher low + break of last lower high That’s CHoCH – early hint that bears are losing it. Use it to: - Stop over-trading against evolving structure - Start tracking potential rotation zones - Build a more mature, patient game instead of full degen mode Price always speaks. CHoCH is just learning to hear the first word before the whole speech.

articleBody: After the Middle East conflict started on 28 February, with no end in sight, commodity prices are expected to continue rising, leading to inflation. Why did precious metals not perform as expected by most retail investors? The savvy ones know their time may come later. The immediate concern is the upcoming inflation data and the possibility of Kevin Warsh becoming the next Fed Chair. He is considered hawkish, believing that "inflation is the enemy," compared to the more dovish Jerome Powell, who prefers to "let the economy breathe." Kevin Warsh’s first key decision could come in June, and inflation numbers may not look encouraging. Market watchers expect him to hike interest rates, and this could just be the beginning if the Middle East conflict becomes prolonged. Investors are anticipating higher interest rates, which could lead to a stronger US dollar. Gold, also known as a dollar hedge, may face pressure under this expectation. Therefore, it is not surprising to see precious metals being suppressed for the time being. Why are they ranging for now? Inflation was already sticky, and this situation could become a launchpad for prices to move higher with any trigger, especially events like those on 28 February. If inflation and interest rates continue to rise, the US dollar’s strength may eventually face limitations due to deficits, debt, and other structural issues. When investors realise it may not be worth chasing a premium in the US dollar while inflation continues rising, crude oil and commodities may continue their uptrend, allowing precious metals to reclaim their crown. For now, traders may look to benefit from the range. articleBody: If you look at Bitcoin from the very first cycles - you will see that the behavior is surprisingly consistent Every time price reaches the area between the red and purple arcs - it has historically marked a cycle top. After that we always saw a brutal correction — roughly 80% drawdown. No exceptions so far Another interesting detail: the time from one ATH to the next ATH has been very similar each cycle, around 1,491 days. After each major bottom price didn’t just bounce — it built a cup-shaped structure, slowly accumulating before the next expansion At the same time every cycle produced higher lows almost like steps going up (levels 1–5) So! If we get a deep correction - the next major bottom could land around $29k–$39k If the correction is less severe - support shifts higher to $39k–$51k Additionally, price may first return to the previous ATH - similar to what was in 2021, potentially pushing slightly above it. This would be consistent with historical behavior, where prior ATH are often reclaimed or marginally exceeded before a larger macro pullback unfolds. If the current cup plays out the same way it did in previous cycles, the measured move points to a potential Bitcoin price around $361k at the next ATH No indicators, no hype — just repeating structure, time symmetry and proportional moves that Bitcoin has respected for years

articleBody: Bitcoin intraday price action has now found acceptance below the $72,600 resistance, a key high-timeframe level that previously acted as a strong supply zone. This shift is significant, as what was once support has now been flipped into resistance, indicating a bearish change in short-term structure. Following this breakdown, price is likely to retest the $72,600 region, which will serve as a critical level to determine the next move. If this retest occurs with weak or declining volume, it increases the probability of a bearish rejection, confirming the level as resistance. From a technical perspective, a failed retest would signal that sellers remain in control, opening the door for a continued downside rotation. The next key level to watch in this scenario is the $65,000 support, which represents a major high-timeframe demand zone. This potential move would align with typical market behaviour, where price seeks liquidity at lower levels after losing a key resistance. Traders should monitor volume closely at the retest, as lack of bullish participation strengthens the bearish case, while a strong reclaim above $72,600 would invalidate this outlook.

articleBody: Cycle Analysis: The 2025 Peak is In, What’s Next? The provided chart illustrates the clockwork precision of Bitcoin’s multi-year cycles. For the second time in history, Bitcoin has followed its ~1,064-day rally pattern from bottom to peak, successfully tagging the curved resistance before entering a scheduled cooling period. Historical Cycle Breakdown 🔹 2013 Peak: A ~731-day rally post-2011 bottom, ending in a massive blow-off top and a deep multi-year winter. 🔹2017 Peak: An 853-day surge from the 2015 bottom, resulting in a 12,000% gain and a subsequent 84% drawdown. 🔹2021 Peak: A perfect 1,064-day rally from the 2018 floor, gaining 2,000% before a 76% correction. 🔹2025 Peak: Consistent with the 1,064-day theory, Bitcoin rallied from the November 2022 bottom to a late-2025 peak, gaining over 700%. Current Bear Market Status (March 2026) As of March 19, 2026, Bitcoin is trading at $70,095. The "Bubble" phase of 2025 has concluded, and we are now roughly halfway through the typical one-year correction window. The Correction Timeline: Based on the consistent 364-day historical pattern from peak to trough, this bear market is projected to find its bottom around October 2026. Target Bottom Zone: Following the trend of diminishing drawdowns (84% → 76%), a 60–70% correction from the 2025 peak would place the potential October bottom in the $50K–$60K range. Conclusion Bitcoin is currently following its historical "script" with high precision. With the 2025 peak behind us, the focus has shifted to capital preservation and accumulation. Historical data suggests that while the current $70K level is a significant psychological floor, the true cycle bottom is likely to materialise around October 2026. Patience and risk management will be key as the market grinds toward this final liquidity reset before the next multi-year ascent begins.

articleBody: Bitcoin is once again forming a familiar pattern — a series of impulsive drops followed by weak rising consolidations. If you zoom out, this isn’t just one setup. It’s a fractal sequence of bearish continuation patterns: • Sharp sell-off • Rising channel / wedge • Lower high • Breakdown This structure has now repeated multiple times, and price is currently testing the upper boundary of yet another rising channel. What stands out: Each bounce is getting weaker Lower highs continue to form Momentum is flattening during consolidation This suggests that buyers are absorbing less supply each cycle, while sellers remain in control. Current Setup: Price is approaching resistance within the local channel. If this structure holds, the most likely path is: → Rejection from channel resistance → Breakdown below local support → Continuation toward lower liquidity zones Key Level to Watch: The mid-range support (~current consolidation base). A clean break below that level would confirm continuation. Invalidation: Strong breakout above the channel Reclaim of prior lower high Momentum expansion to the upside If that happens, the bearish structure fails. Conclusion: This is not a random move — it’s a pattern repeating itself across multiple legs. Until structure changes, the trend remains bearish.

articleBody: PRICe delivered a clean sell-side liquidity sweep below the external range low (ERL), taking out weak hands and triggering stops. This move signals a shift in order flow as the market transitions from discount back toward premium. Following the sweep, price began forming higher lows and showing internal strength, suggesting accumulation after manipulation. With sell-side liquidity now taken, the logical next objective is buy-side liquidity, sitting in the premium range (IRL). The current narrative is straightforward: Liquidity taken → displacement → continuation toward IRL 🧠 Key Concepts: ERL (External Range Liquidity) = previous swing high/low IRL (Internal Range Liquidity) = inefficiencies / FVG targets Bias flips bullish after sell-side sweep 🎯 Trade Idea: Bias: Bullish Entry: Discount zone after sweep (~69.3k–69.8k) Stop Loss: Below ERL low (~68.8k) Target: IRL / premium (~74.5k–75.5k) ⚡ Summary: Market cleared liquidity on the downside and is now positioned to rebalance inefficiencies higher. As long as price holds above the swept low, continuation toward premium remains the most probable path.

articleBody: Bitcoin has again reacted from the 71,000 – 71,300 demand zone, validating the previous structure after the drop driven by macro pressure from the Federal Reserve and stronger USD. This zone continues to act as a key liquidity base, where buyers are stepping in after the impulsive rally and CHOCH formation. Technically, this still looks like a healthy pullback within a bullish structure, not a full reversal. The market swept liquidity below support and tapped into demand, which often signals accumulation before continuation. If this zone holds again, BTC can build momentum toward 72,800 → 73,500, and potentially retest higher resistance near 74k. However, if price fails to hold 71,000, it would indicate weakness and lack of buying strength. In that case, the market could seek deeper liquidity at the next major demand zone (69,200 – 69,600), which aligns with the previous consolidation base and stronger institutional interest area. Fundamentally, BTC remains sensitive to risk sentiment and liquidity conditions. While geopolitical tensions support crypto in the bigger picture, short-term moves are still heavily influenced by interest rates and USD strength. articleBody: The chart shows a strong upward movement followed by a sharp decline from the recent peak. On the left side, price previously experienced a rapid rise and then a steep fall inside the highlighted red zone, indicating a period of intense volatility before stabilizing. After this phase, the market recovered and formed another upward move that reached a new high. From that high, price began a sustained downward movement, forming consecutive lower highs and lower lows. A horizontal projection from the earlier structure points toward a lower grey zone, suggesting a measured move from the prior range. The market has now moved significantly closer to this zone after breaking below intermediate support levels. The grey rectangle at the bottom marks a key reaction area where price previously interacted with the market. The green marker highlights the expected point of interest within this zone. Overall, the structure illustrates a bearish expansion targeting a lower support region after distribution near the top. Speculative Outlook The projected path suggests that price may continue declining toward the highlighted lower zone. If the downward momentum persists, the market could reach the marked area where the green marker indicates a potential reaction point. Upon arrival, price may stabilize and produce a bounce as buyers respond to the lower level. This could result in a temporary upward move after the extended sell-off. However, if selling pressure remains dominant even within the zone, the market may break below it and continue moving lower. In that case, the highlighted area would act as a pause rather than a reversal point. For now, the structure suggests continuation toward the lower zone, followed by a possible reaction depending on how price behaves once it reaches that region.

articleBody: Everything is just 0s and 1s This chart is meant to simplify the Elliott Wave Theory and project future price estimates. Starting with the 4 Year cycle we can see that it'll be there forever simply because its the universal law on how everything works. The Fibonaccian spiral, Fractals, and so on. It may feel like we should have not topped based on systems like Pi Cycle, S2F, etc. Perhaps those systems dont call for the universal law. Similar to using a indicator in real time and realizing the signals start to change to what you see in the past as time stretches. The only indicator that stays consistent for calling Tops/Bottoms is the 50 Week MA/EMA. The (0) 1 - 2 - 3 Pattern Going back to the first Gen adopters of Bitcoin price created this 3 - Drive Impulse pattern which kept the lower trendline intact and the upper trendline as well until we saw a break below that would have been the 3rd touch to send price even higher. This alone tells me we had some type of reset, this is where '0' comes back into play starting the whole '3 Drive - Impulse' all over again. Today we're back at '3' and price is crashing while standing at 4 years again. The lower trendline doesn't look like it'll hold, and its very important that it does if you want to see a blow off top this year. Also not considering ALTs like NASDAQ:RAY and CRYPTOCAP:SOL had major structural damage. Hypothetically lets say price breaks below and we land somewhere near $30k. At this point the pattern has come to a reset and we do it all over again. 0, 1, 2, 3 If this does play out, on a realistic point I could see a price target of around $300k - $500k by 2030 And maybe $800k - $1M by 2034. A lengthening cycle like this will shake out people who keep buying tops and quitting at bottoms. Real players will be well rewarded for another decade/4yr cycle to come. This is just a theory and will only be proven when time tells. The main focus is to navigate whats already in front of us.

articleBody: The chart tells a clean story. BTC has been testing the same resistance zone — the golden band at $73,000–$74,500 — three times since early March. Each test has been rejected. That zone is where sellers are consistently showing up. Below price, two levels are doing the structural work: The descending AVWAP (purple line) anchored from the January high at $96k has now curved upward and sits at $69,471. Price is above it — that is why the bias reads BULLISH. This line was resistance for the entire February selloff. It is now support. As long as BTC holds above it, the structure is intact. Below that, the red horizontal at $68,000 is the hard floor. That level absorbed the February lows and the early March retests. Two touches, held both times. Current price $70,449 sits in the middle of this structure — above AVWAP support, below the supply zone. The post-Fed pullback from $74,500 brought it back into this range. That is not a breakdown. That is the market digesting the Fed's hawkish repricing in the same $69–74k range it has held for three weeks. The levels that matter: $74,500 — break and close above this on volume = next leg $69,471 — AVWAP support, the line the bull case depends on $68,000 — hard floor, lose this and the structure changes Until $74,500 breaks convincingly, this is a range. The bias is bullish because VWAP Dev is positive and the AVWAP is below price — but the supply zone is doing its job. Patience over conviction here.

Below that, the red horizontal at $68,000 is the hard floor. That level absorbed the February lows and the early March retests. Two touches, held both times. Current price $70,449 sits in the middle of this structure — above AVWAP support, below the supply zone. The post-Fed pullback from $74,500 brought it back into this range. That is not a breakdown. That is the market digesting the Fed's hawkish repricing in the same $69–74k range it has held for three weeks. The levels that matter: $74,500 — break and close above this on volume = next leg $69,471 — AVWAP support, the line the bull case depends on $68,000 — hard floor, lose this and the structure changes Until $74,500 breaks convincingly, this is a range. The bias is bullish because VWAP Dev is positive and the AVWAP is below price — but the supply zone is doing its job. Patience over conviction here.

articleBody: BTC opened the week with displacement — pushing to $74K after weeks of slow grinding. The structure has shifted and we're now approaching the level that will define the rest of March. [b]Structure recap:[/b] The 4H has flipped bullish. Starting from the $66K CHoCH, price has printed a series of BOS confirmations — each one validating the structural shift. This isn't a slow grind anymore, it's displacement with intent. [b]The decision zone — $77–77.4K:[/b] There's a major HTF supply OB sitting at $77–$77.4K from the February distribution. This is the zone where institutions were selling before the breakdown. It hasn't been tested since — first touch of unmitigated supply is where we find out if this move is real. Break through $77.4K with displacement = structural shift confirmed. Path opens to $83K+ where the next supply sits. Rejection at $77K with LTF CHoCH = the move up was a liquidity grab. Price likely rotates back into the range and the $69K equal lows below become the target. [b]What's supporting the bulls:[/b] The CHoCH at $66K is the structural anchor — bullish as long as that holds. Multiple BOS on the 4H show sustained buying, not a single spike. Demand OB at $72.8–$73K is the nearest support if we get a pullback before the $77K test. [b]What's supporting the bears:[/b] HTF trend is still technically bearish until $77.4K gets reclaimed. Last week's staircase grind built a wall of breakout longs with stops below — that liquidity hasn't been taken. A rejection from $77K supply creates the perfect setup for a sweep of the $69K lows. [b]What matters:[/b] How price reacts at $77K tells us everything. Displacement through = bullish. Rejection with structure break = bearish. Everything between here and there is just positioning. Structure first. Always.

Did they use the $74,000 fakeout to escape? No. A deep-scan of the historical ledger shows the main positions peaked at 79,115 BTC. Today, it sits at 78,554 BTC. The $5.5 Billion anvil is still entirely intact, strapped directly to the market's ankle. 3. THE TECHNICAL BATTLEGROUND (The Squeeze) The market is currently hovering around $70,480. It looks like it is "holding support," but the structural reality is a highly condensed algorithmic squeeze. When we pull the Fibonacci retracement from the $65,700 low up to the $76,000 top, the hidden grid of the market is perfectly revealed: The Ceiling: The 0.500 Fib sits at $70,850, acting as heavy overhead resistance. The Floor: The 0.618 Golden Pocket sits at $69,634. Right through the exact middle of this squeeze runs the 4-Hour 100 Moving Average at $70,583. The algorithms are intentionally trapping the price inside this tiny corridor. Every time the price dips to the 0.618 Golden Pocket, retail algorithms blindly "buy the dip." Every time it hits the 0.500 ceiling, Wall Street sells into it. They are letting retail day-traders chop themselves to pieces against the moving averages while Wall Street quietly lets gravity take over. 4. THE PHANTOM VERDICT: WHAT COMES NEXT The artificial +30 premium floor has been removed. The market is now in the "Slow Bleed" phase, drifting downward under the weight of organic retail exhaustion. The true structural defence line right now is that 0.618 Golden Pocket at $69,634. As the American premium remains dead and negative, that support level will eventually fracture. When they finally run out of margin and the exchange algorithms are forced to market-sell those 78,554 Bitcoins into an empty order book, the trapdoor will officially open. Phantorians, you just successfully navigated one of the most perfectly executed institutional traps of the year. You didn't buy the top, and you didn't provide Wall Street with their exit liquidity. articleBody: Bitcoin is currently trading around $71,000 after a strong recovery from the February lows. However, price is now showing signs of weakness as it approaches a key resistance trendline. The recent rejection suggests that the market may be transitioning from a bullish correction into a more cautious or potentially bearish phase. The current area is critical, as it will determine whether BTC can sustain its recovery or roll over again. [b]Last Time[/b] Looking at previous price action, BTC formed a very similar structure earlier this year. Price pushed above the trendline, creating a false breakout (fake-out), before quickly reversing and breaking below the bullish daily FVG. That move led to a sharp downside expansion. This historical setup is important because the current structure is closely mirroring that behavior, increasing the probability of a similar outcome if key levels fail. [b]Fake-out[/b] BTC has recently performed a fake-out above the ascending trendline, briefly breaking above resistance before getting rejected. This type of price action often traps breakout traders and signals exhaustion in the move. Following the rejection, price is now drifting back down toward support, indicating that buyers are losing momentum. Fake-outs like this typically precede either consolidation or a stronger move in the opposite direction. [b]Daily FVG[/b] Price is currently hovering around the daily FVG, which is acting as a key support zone. This level is crucial for maintaining any bullish structure. As long as BTC holds this imbalance, there is still a chance for stabilization or another push higher. However, if price breaks and closes below this daily FVG, it would signal weakness and likely trigger a continuation to the downside, similar to the previous occurrence. [b]Final Thoughts[/b] Bitcoin is at a pivotal level, with the daily FVG acting as the line between bullish continuation and bearish reversal. The recent fake-out above resistance adds caution, especially given the similarity to past price action. If this support fails to hold, BTC is likely to move lower and revisit deeper levels. Holding this zone, however, could keep the market in consolidation, but overall, the risk is starting to tilt to the downside.

articleBody: The title says it all. We've been tracking TRXUSDT and how it moves above each moving average. First, EMA55, then EMA89 and now the longer-period MA200, the most important indicator for the Cryptocurrency market. There was a rejection last time right above MA200, 18-January. TRXUSDT moved down in order to build-up strength. This process is over and now we see a second break and challenge of this level as resistance—black line on the chart. Sometimes it can happens that there is another rejection and the third one is the win. I don't think this will happen now. I think MA200 has been conquered as resistance and this means so much. Above MA200, long-term growth is possible, but some context is needed. If the action is happening at bottom prices, after a strong bear-market. MA200 comes down and then the action moves above, long-term growth is possible. Here we have a simple correction and prices are trading high-up. This is still extremely bullish but instead of 6 months or more of bullish action confirmed, we go with 3-6 months based on the context and chart structure. TRXUSDT (Tron) moving above MA200 confirms a very strong bullish bias. As Tron grows, the rest of the market does the same. We are seeing growth on Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, Cardano, Dogecoin and the smaller altcoins as well. Actually, the best signal is coming from the smaller altcoins. Finally, we are seeing 2-3 digits bullish breakout on project after project, and this confirms the process of a marketwide bull-run. In the past few months, there were always some projects breaking up but only one or two, and then it always went quiet—the market. Now we see several projects breaking out with force, then a new set, then another set and so on. This is the final signal that supports marketwide bullish action. Such a process can last 3 weeks at the worst, or 2-3 months. The change in sentiment, market conditions and life conditions is so huge that is hard to explain. It is not something that can be realized through words. I can tell you about it over and over and you might even fully agree with me; but, it is not the same to live through the experience as reading about it. The energy changes. There is an actual change in the energy, the feeling, the atmosphere. The way the people communicate, the market, the comments, the interactions. When was the last time you saw hundreds of bulls sharing positive news, comments and information? A very long time ago. If you were to review the comments section, you only see hate, doubt and insults; this is the bearish period, this is what is meant by "extreme fear" on the market sentiment. When the market turns truly green, not by price action but a human realization; when people start to feel the change, in their pockets, in their hearts, in their minds and in their heads, then everything changes. I am looking forward to this moment. It is harder on me than anything you can imagine. This signal on TRXUSDT gives further strength to what I've been sharing with you for months. articleBody: As you can see, ever since failing to make it out of the Wick, Bitcoin fist started to consolidate and then heavily dropped in price, from 76,000 to 68,800, reaching the 4H FVG Low, but it bounced back and closed inside the 4H FVG. Afterwards, it started to recover a bit, currently being traded at around 70,600. BTCUSDT retested the 2H FVG, but it dropped back down again, currently it's stuck between the 2 FVGs. So far, the High of the 4H FVG has been acting as an support for Bitcoin, but it has to go through the 2H FVG next which is a strong resistance zone right now. I expect BTC to retest the 2H FVG, and if it goes above the FVG's C.E. at 71,720 and stabilizes there, we could see it to go to retest the Wick, at 72,945. But if it drops and reaches the 4H FVG and the support fails, we could see it reaching 68,800 and a bit lower to sweep the Liquidity below the 68,793 level.
